beats.reasoning: The SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon likely adds uncertainty, so he leaves early.

beats.activity: Deep work debugging level flow and smoothing UI interactions while the rain keeps the mood low-key.
beats.reasoning: He’s hyper-focused and rain naturally makes him stick to indoor tasks during the busiest closure window.

diary: Grey, drizzly Tuesday again—the kind that makes the whole world feel muted. I woke up at home in Bothell, poured coffee, and did that slow rainy-morning reset where you pretend you have all day. The SR-520 bridge closure eastbound from 9am to noon was the big headline, so I planned my drive like a puzzle: extra buffer, calmer route, no heroics. Leaving around 7:55, the cross-lake traffic felt thicker than it should, but at least I wasn’t rushing. At the indie studio, I disappeared into debugging—tuning level flow and smoothing those UI interactions until they finally stopped feeling “almost right.” Lunch at Caffé Vita in downtown Kirkland kept me dry, and later I grabbed snacks at Safeway on Everett Ave. After work, I ended up near Lake Union Park, walking in the drizzle, then grabbed dinner from the familiar food truck rotation at Pike Place Market. By the time I got home, I was warm, slightly tired, and weirdly hopeful—like the rain was just another asset I could work with.
thought_process: I chose to lean into the weather instead of fighting it—staying disciplined with timing because SR-520’s closure was real friction, not just inconvenience. That let me protect my deep work block in the morning, when my brain actually likes the hard problems (debugging level flow, chasing UI jank). When the day started dragging under the grey sky, I kept breaks simple: indoor lunch at Caffé Vita so I wouldn’t lose momentum, then quick errands at Safeway so testing runs later could be uninterrupted. The mood shift happened mid-afternoon during the Lake Union Park walk; seeing the clouds hang low made everything feel less urgent and more solvable. World events didn’t change my goals, but the travel disruption definitely shaped my patience and how I paced myself.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
